behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is in module handoff (ingest/parse/correlate) rather than a single pure function. Higher levels were rejected because the behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess or any AI/ML output; it only specifies cross-service grouping semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run the in-process pipeline with mixed logs from multiple services sharing and not sharing trace IDs; assert grouped output contains all entries for each trace ID across services with no cross-trace contamination.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily ordering logic across parser/correlation/timeline modules, where inter-module data interpretation can fail even if unit tests pass. System was rejected because behavior text does not require real external contract checks or real infra round-trip; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by no AI steps.
behaviors.test_description: Feed logs from multiple services with controlled timestamp offsets up to 500ms into the assembled local pipeline and verify rendered timeline order matches expected skew-tolerant sequence.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: Risk is in combined baseline computation, percentile aggregation, and report flag propagation across modules. Higher levels were considered but rejected: behavior text does not require real external API/OS/subprocess validation, and no AI/ML judgment is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the local analytics pipeline on synthetic windows with known baseline and injected high-latency tail; assert p99 threshold logic triggers exactly when p99 exceeds 3x baseline and appears in anomaly report output.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans parser error handling, pipeline resilience, and reporting counter consistency across modules. System was rejected because no explicit real external contract/round-trip is required by the text; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified (no model dependency).
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline with a mixed batch of valid and malformed JSON lines; assert process continues, valid entries are processed, malformed entries are skipped, and reported skipped count matches malformed input count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly depends on real-time throughput and backpressure characteristics, which are sensitive to runtime/OS scheduling and process behavior. Integration was rejected because in-process fakes can hide timing, queueing, and resource-contention failures; this needs end-to-end burst generation and observed output counts/lag.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual tool process under load with a real burst generator at 10,000 entries/sec for a fixed interval; verify ingested count equals produced/correlated count (no drops) and end-to-end processing lag stays within defined non-falling-behind threshold.
